title: implement D2 (inline doctrine, demote/drop §x across lead-architect/lead-po/bc-reviewer) and D3 (extract turn-limited exchange to PDR-014 skill)
notes:
mechanism_observation: sweep exceeded msg enumeration (lead-po §3.3, bc-reviewer §4.4×2); line nums drifted; delivery needs version bump+tag — Three load-bearing observations about the self-contained-templates contract surface (ADR-037 WS-4):

1. **Scope was BROADER than the message's line-by-line enumeration.** The dispatch enumerated lead-architect (6 sites) and lead-po §3.2 only. The criterion-based acceptance pin required sweeping ALL role templates, which surfaced TWO additional dangling-citation sites the enumeration omitted: lead-po **§3.3** (the 'artifacts collapse onto §3.3' line) and bc-reviewer **§4.4 ×2** (the Reviewer→lead clarify-loop refs). Both were dangling external citations identical in kind to the enumerated ones. The lead's contract-surface model should record that the resolution swept every role template (lead-architect, lead-po, bc-reviewer; bc-implementer was already clean), not just the enumerated lines — otherwise a future 'self-contained' audit re-derives the same misses.

2. **The message's enumerated line numbers had drifted.** They were captured from 'shop-templates show' on 2026-06-12 and were stale against the base (e.g. §3.4 turn-limited at message-L92 was actually ~L105; §6 at message-L266/346 vs actual ~L279/368). Enumerations keyed on line numbers rot between capture and dispatch; criterion-based pins (grep predicate, not line list) are the durable contract form and is what this work followed.

3. **Delivery currency is a SEPARATE step not in this work_id's acceptance.** The new PDR-014 skill (po-architect-decomposition-exchange) plus the template edits reach ALREADY-LAUNCHED lead instances only after a package version bump + tag (the lead-slkk pattern). This work_id's acceptance is the canonical change + its tests only; I did NOT bump the version or push a release tag. The lead must track the version-bump+tag delivery as a distinct WS-4 step so launched leads actually receive the self-contained templates and the new skill.
close_reason: GREEN: D2 inlined doctrine + dropped/demoted all §x/findings; D3 PDR-014 skill (commit 359496e); integrated edca834

title: transform canonical lead.md to GREEN: reposition PRIME DIRECTIVE before SHOP_NAME heading (de-dup), replace choice-suppression body with 3-trigger positive reframe
notes:
GREEN: edit src/shop_templates/templates/claude/lead.md to the acceptance-pin end state
mechanism_observation: lead-f3gm DELTA EVIDENCE stale vs canonical lead.md; pins still genuinely unmet, reached idempotently — The request_bugfix DELTA EVIDENCE for lead-f3gm described a pre-state that did NOT match canonical templates/claude/lead.md on origin/main at dispatch (base 2872e61):

1. It claimed Block 1 (PRIME DIRECTIVE) was 'ENTIRELY ABSENT' and that 'shop-templates show lead' would show no opening block. In fact a verbatim-matching '# PRIME DIRECTIVE — act, do not ask' block ALREADY EXISTED — but MISPOSITIONED, after the '# {{SHOP_NAME}} — lead shop instructions' heading (~line 7), not opening the file.

2. It claimed Block 2 (choice-suppression) carried the 'OLDER, WEAKER prohibition-framed' body. In fact it was an INTERMEDIATE positive reframe (commit 2730ea0 had already partially reframed it): the body opened 'Per the **PRIME DIRECTIVE**, when a procedural choice arises...' with a 'Carve-out' framing and only TWO triggers (Scope or vocabulary; PO/Architect routing) — missing the third trigger, the DECIDE-AND-ACT lead, and the positive-standing-order closer.

The acceptance pins were NONETHELESS genuinely unmet, so the fix was REAL, not a no-op: pin 1 (position) was violated (block after the heading, not before) and pin 2 was violated (intermediate body lacked the 'DECIDE EVERY OPERATIONAL QUESTION YOURSELF AND ACT ON IT' lead, the third 'Unauthorized outward-facing action' trigger, and the closer). I reached the pins IDEMPOTENTLY by inspecting the actual file first and transforming to the end state (move+de-dup PD before heading; replace choice-suppression body), rather than blind-inserting per the stale pre-state.

Load-bearing for WS-4 part 2+: the lead's contract-surface model (its in-product .claude/canonical/lead-primer.md copy and 'shop-templates show lead' expectation) had drifted ahead of what it believed canonical carried. Future request_bugfix DELTA EVIDENCE for canonical lead.md should be recomputed against actual package data (read_claude_md_primer('lead')) at compose time, not against the lead's local primer copy, or the next dispatch may describe a pre-state that is already partly satisfied.
close_reason: GREEN: repositioned PRIME DIRECTIVE to open lead.md (de-dup, exactly one); replaced choice-suppression body with 3-trigger positive reframe; feat 4f99a62
